title: Ransomware Attack
description:
A sequence of high-severity alerts from multiple endpoints.
These alerts were observed to be a range of coordinated malicious activities involving:
Suspicious binaries executed from user TEMP directories; Encoded Powershell activity on user workstations; Multiple Outbound connections, Administrative authentication, Clearing of windows event logs on compromised hosts; Rapid spike in file modification on central server and Endpoint protection detections on staging binaries
tasks:
[{"createdBy": "responder18@irorg.com", "createdAt": "06/05/2026 14:50", "title": "Detection", "description": "=== TASK ACTIVITY HISTORY ===

--- LOG ENTRY ---
ID: ~40980688
Author: responder18@irorg.com
Date: 06/05/2026 15:21
Type: case_task_log
Message: Incident timeline: 11 high alerts were observed within a short period of time, 19 minutes in total between 9:12:33 to 9:31:03
The alerts showed malicious process creation; Execution of dropper binary, suspicious logon on the domain controller, deletion of windows event logs and then connection establishment to an external ip address on the web server.
The incident began with host ws-user03, then spread to ws-user04 and then to the domain controller dc01 and finally to the server srv-web01", "owner": "responder18@irorg.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "06/05/2026 15:03", "endDate": "06/05/2026 15:21"}, {"createdBy": "responder18@irorg.com", "createdAt": "06/05/2026 14:51", "title": "Analysis", "description": "=== TASK ACTIVITY HISTORY ===

--- LOG ENTRY ---
ID: ~81948896
Author: responder17@irorg.com
Date: 06/05/2026 15:52
Type: case_task_log
Message: At 9:13 am a powershell script was executed from the same host and a process %TEMP%\svchost32.exe was then created.
At 9:17 am a communicated was discovered to the destination IP address 88.119.174.129 which has been discovered to be associated with the RYUK ransomware attack. And was flagged as malicious on Virustotal. The user than cleared the log on that system. 

The user then logged into the DC with admin credentials and from there logged in the the server srv-web01.irorg.local  which was compromised as a connection to a mailicious domain was discovered.

The attacked then executed the df.exe executable which led to the encryption of the system. The backup server was also compromised by the attacker.

Based on analysis it is likely ryuk ransomeware attack.


--- LOG ENTRY ---
ID: ~28856
Author: responder17@irorg.com
Date: 06/05/2026 15:30
Type: case_task_log
Message: ![![![From analysing the log "host=ws-user03.irorg.local src_ip=10.0.1.21 event=process_creation process="%TEMP%\update_213.exe" hash=2cd01dab44247dee9c0f9511b3b1d11d5a69afcc87efc59446238021d5343aad description="Dropper binary executed on user workstation"

Investigations shows that at 9:12 am on 24th February 2026 an attacker got access to a user system ws-user03.irorg.local with IP address 10.0.1.21

A maliciuos file update_213.exe was executed from the temp directory which is a suspiciuos activity.

An investigation with virus total show that the file hash has been discovered to be malicious across multiple platforms.]()]()]()

ID: ~81981584
Author: responder17@irorg.com
Date: 06/05/2026 15:36
Type: case_task_log
Message: host=ws-user03.irorg.local event=network_connection dst_domain=servicewikii.com description="Connection attempt to suspected C2 domain."

From the above log a communication was discovered from the user ws-user03.irorg.local to the domain servicewikii.com which from the MISP platform has been discovered to be malicious and associated with the "RYUK ransomeware" which indicates the attack is possibly a ransomeware attack. It was flagged as a suspicious domain by the security tools.

Message: Lesson Learned

Integrate threat intelligence to firewall to help with detection and blocking of discovered malicious addresses.
